There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) in the source document, and none has been added to the output.
The single-page source document is represented in its entirety in the output. All 'WHEREAS' clauses and the 'NOW, THEREFORE' resolution are included.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison reveals that all substantive text from the resolution, including the specific legislation names and the names of the signatories, is preserved without significant omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output accurately reflects the wording of the source document. Normalization of whitespace and the use of superscript for the date ordinal ('th' in 6th) are within the bounds of acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: Each distinct text block in the source document—the location header, the resolution title, the five 'WHEREAS' recitals, the 'RESOLVED' clause, the date line, and the two signature blocks—is represented exactly once in the output JSON. No content duplication is detected.

The output JSON promotes the primary document title to a section heading. As there are no further subheadings or nested sections in the original document, the resulting hierarchy is logical and flat, ensuring accessibility for screen readers.
The source document is a text-based resolution and contains no tables. Consequently, no table structural errors exist in the output.

The output JSON preserves the logical top-to-bottom reading order found in the source PDF. It correctly sequences the identifying information, legal recitals, the resolution statement, the execution date, and the attestation signatures.
The source document does not contain bulleted or numbered lists. The sequence of 'WHEREAS' clauses has been appropriately rendered as individual paragraphs, preserving their semantic meaning.
The source document contains no URLs or contact links. The output JSON accurately reflects this absence, with no injected or fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual layout of the document is faithfully represented. The source PDF is a standard single-column legal resolution, and the HTML output maintains this structure. Although the HTML contains a plain-text version followed by a styled version, the styled version correctly preserves the visual organization of the source, including centered text for headings, signature line alignments, and the positioning of the county seal. The visual flow for a reader remains consistent with the source document's intent.

The source document does not contain any tables. It consists entirely of hierarchical text (headings, 'WHEREAS' clauses, 'RESOLVED' clause) and signature blocks. No table-related rendering issues were observed.

All meaningful visual elements from the source PDF are correctly present in the HTML output. Specifically, the handwritten signatures of Mark F. Richardson (Chairman) and Susan O. Washburn (Clerk to the Board) are rendered as images, as is the official seal of Rockingham County. These elements are located in the signature block at the bottom of the styled document section, matching their placement in the source.

There is significant visual duplication of content within the HTML output. The entire text of the resolution—from the geographical header ('NORTH CAROLINA ROCKINGHAM COUNTY') through the entire body of clauses down to the signature lines—is rendered twice on a single page. The first instance appears as a simplified plain-text block, and the second instance appears as a styled block mimicking the original layout. Furthermore, the main title of the resolution ('RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION...') is extracted and placed at the very top of the page as a primary heading, resulting in that specific text string appearing three separate times.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a single, non-repeating instance of the resolution text.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the content block from 'NORTH CAROLINA...' to 'CLERK TO THE BOARD' appearing twice. The first instance is a left-aligned text block, and the second is a centered block with images. The title 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING...' appears as a top header, in the first block, and in the second block.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the entire document rendering in the HTML output.
